title: scanner.rs: UNIQUE constraint detection uses fragile error string matching
description:
scanner.rs lines 576-584: is_unique_constraint() detects UNIQUE violations by string-matching the SQLite error message (.contains('UNIQUE constraint failed') etc). SQLite error message text is not stable API.

## Algorithm
1. Open wallet/src/scanner.rs, find is_unique_constraint() function (lines ~576-584)
2. Replace the function body:
   - Use e.downcast_ref::<sqlx::Error>() to extract the underlying sqlx error from anyhow::Error
   - Match on sqlx::Error::Database(db_err)
   - Return db_err.is_unique_violation() (uses sqlx 0.8 ErrorKind enum, stable API)
   - Return false for any non-Database sqlx error
3. Check existing imports in scanner.rs for sqlx — add 'use sqlx;' if not present
4. Update the test unique_constraint_detection() (lines ~766-772):
   - Current test creates a fake anyhow error with a string — this does not test the real code path
   - New test: use an in-memory WalletStore (or call insert_note twice for same (txid, output_index)) to produce a real sqlx UNIQUE constraint error, then assert is_unique_constraint(&e) returns true
   - Also assert is_unique_constraint(&anyhow::anyhow!("some other error")) returns false

## Defensive programming rules
- The downcast_ref pattern must handle the anyhow wrapping correctly — do NOT unwrap
- Return false (not panic) for any non-matching error shape
- No key material in any new log statement

## Acceptance
- cargo test -p nie-wallet passes including unique_constraint_detection test using real sqlx error
- cargo clippy -p nie-wallet -- -D warnings passes
- No string literals 'UNIQUE constraint' or 'unique constraint' remain in is_unique_constraint()
